# Citations
<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

All sources visited during research on 2026-03-20 via WebSearch. Sources are numbered sequentially and referenced as `[N]` throughout the deliverable and reference files.

---

## Controlled Experiments & Peer-Reviewed Research

**[1]** Peng, S., Kalliamvakou, E., Cihon, P., & Demirer, M. (2023). "The Impact of AI on Developer Productivity: Evidence from GitHub Copilot." arXiv:2302.06590.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.06590
- **Data extracted:** 55.8% faster task completion (71 min vs 161 min), N=95 developers, p=0.0017, CI [21%, 89%]. Less experienced developers benefited more.
- **Quality:** Peer-reviewed RCT. Single task type (greenfield JS HTTP server). GitHub/Microsoft affiliated.

**[2]** Cui, Z.K., Demirer, M., Jaffe, S., Musolff, L., Peng, S., & Salz, T. (2024/2026). "The Effects of Generative AI on High-Skilled Work: Evidence from Three Field Experiments with Software Developers." *Management Science*, DOI: 10.1287/mnsc.2025.00535.
- **URL:** https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4945566
- **Data extracted:** 26.08% increase in weekly completed tasks (SE: 10.3%), 13.55% more commits, 38.38% more builds. N=4,867 developers across Microsoft, Accenture, Fortune 100. Junior devs: 35-39% speedup; senior devs: 8-16%.
- **Quality:** Three RCTs but individually underpowered. Low compliance at Microsoft (8.5% initial sign-up).

**[3]** Becker, J., Rush, N., Barnes, E., & Rein, D. (2025). "Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity." METR. arXiv:2507.09089.
- **URL:** https://metr.org/blog/2025-07-10-early-2025-ai-experienced-os-dev-study/
- **Data extracted:** AI increased completion time by 19% (CI: +2% to +39%). Developers predicted 24% speedup, believed 20% speedup post-study. 75% of participants slower with AI. N=16 developers, 246 tasks, $150/hr. Tools: Cursor Pro + Claude 3.5/3.7 Sonnet.
- **Quality:** RCT, small sample. Independent non-profit (METR). Specific to experienced devs on familiar codebases.

**[4]** Google Internal RCT (2024). "How much does AI impact development speed? An enterprise-based randomized controlled trial." arXiv:2410.12944.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2410.12944v2
- **Data extracted:** ~21% faster (96 min vs 114 min), N=96 Google engineers. Not statistically significant after controlling for covariates.
- **Quality:** RCT. Single task (C++ logging feature). No code quality evaluation.

**[5]** Gambacorta, L., Qiu, H., Shan, S., & Rees, D. (2024). "Generative AI and Labour Productivity: A Field Experiment on Coding." BIS Working Papers No. 1208.
- **URL:** https://www.bis.org/publ/work1208.pdf
- **Data extracted:** 55% productivity increase (LOC) for LLM group. Gains statistically significant only among junior staff. Senior: positive but not statistically significant. N=1,219 programmers at Ant Group.
- **Quality:** Field experiment. LOC is a poor productivity proxy.

**[6]** Noy, S. & Zhang, W. (2023). "Experimental Evidence on the Productivity Effects of Generative Artificial Intelligence." *Science*, DOI: 10.1126/science.adh2586.
- **URL:** https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adh2586
- **Data extracted:** 40% time reduction, 18% quality increase, 0.45 SD improvement. N=453 professionals. GPT-3.5 on writing tasks. Low-ability workers benefited more.
- **Quality:** Top journal (Science). Writing tasks, not coding.

**[7]** Weber, T., Brandmaier, M., Schmidt, A., & Mayer, S. (2024). "Significant Productivity Gains through Programming with Large Language Models." *Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction*, Vol. 8, EICS, Article 256.
- **URL:** https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3661145
- **Data extracted:** N=24 programmers, three conditions (baseline, auto-complete, conversational AI). Detailed results behind paywall.
- **Quality:** Peer-reviewed ACM. Small sample.

**[8]** Pavlic, L., Saklamaeva, V., & Beranic, T. (2024). "Can Large-Language Models Replace Humans in Agile Effort Estimation?" *Applied Sciences* (MDPI), 14(24), 12006.
- **URL:** https://www.mdpi.com/2076-3417/14/24/12006
- **Data extracted:** LLM estimation achieved only 16% accuracy. AI useful for task breakdown but not point estimates.
- **Quality:** Controlled experiment. Single tool (GitLab Duo).

**[9]** Bui, T.L., Dam, H.K., & Hoda, R. (2025). "An LLM-based multi-agent framework for agile effort estimation." arXiv:2509.14483.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.14483
- **Data extracted:** SEEAgent multi-agent framework outperforms ML baselines. 75% of practitioners accepted agent estimates. Only 1/3 thought it could fully replace a human.
- **Quality:** arXiv preprint. Evaluated on historical datasets, not live sprints.

**[10]** Spracklen, J. et al. (2025). "We Have a Package for You! A Comprehensive Analysis of Package Hallucinations by Code Generating LLMs." USENIX Security 2025.
- **URL:** https://www.usenix.org/publications/loginonline/we-have-package-you-comprehensive-analysis-package-hallucinations-code
- **Data extracted:** 19.7% of AI-recommended packages are fabricated (440,445 of 2.23M). 576K code samples, 16 models. Commercial models: ~5.2%; open-source: ~21.7%. 58% of hallucinated packages repeat across runs.
- **Quality:** Peer-reviewed (USENIX Security, top venue).

**[11]** arXiv (2025). "Security Vulnerabilities in AI-Generated Code: A Large-Scale Analysis of Public GitHub Repositories." arXiv:2510.26103.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.26103
- **Data extracted:** 7,703 AI-attributed files analyzed. 4,241 CWE instances across 77 vulnerability types. ~12.1% of files had vulnerabilities. Python: 16-18.5%; JS: 8.7-9%.
- **Quality:** arXiv preprint. AI attribution relies on file-level signals.

**[12]** arXiv (2024). "LLM Hallucinations in Practical Code Generation: Phenomena, Mechanism, and Mitigation." arXiv:2409.20550 / ACM SE.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.20550
- **Data extracted:** Identified hallucination types in repository-level code generation. RAG-based mitigation showed consistent effectiveness.
- **Quality:** ACM proceedings (peer-reviewed).

**[13]** ChatGPT Test Case Generation Study (2024). "System Test Case Design from Requirements Specifications." arXiv:2412.03693.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2412.03693v1
- **Data extracted:** 87.7% valid test conditions generated. 72.5% directly useful, 15.2% identified unconsidered edge cases. ChatGPT-4o Turbo, two-stage prompt-chaining.
- **Quality:** Academic. Evaluated against 5 real software projects.

**[14]** Fu & Tantithamthavorn. "GPT2SP: A Transformer-Based Agile Story Point Estimation Approach." *IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering*.
- **URL:** https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9732669/
- **Data extracted:** State-of-the-art baseline for story point estimation. Higher error for cross-project experiments.
- **Quality:** IEEE TSE (top venue).

**[15]** arXiv (2025). "Does AI-Assisted Coding Deliver? A Difference-in-Differences Study of Cursor's Impact." arXiv:2511.04427.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2511.04427v2
- **Data extracted:** 1.84x increase in lines added post-Cursor adoption. Significant increase in static analysis warnings and code complexity.
- **Quality:** Academic. DiD methodology.

**[16]** CatCoder (2025). Repository context improving code generation. arXiv:2406.03283.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2406.03283
- **Data extracted:** Type context improved pass@k scores by up to 17.35% over baselines without repository context.
- **Quality:** Academic (NAACL-adjacent).

**[17]** Georgetown CSET (2024). "Cybersecurity Risks of AI-Generated Code."
- **URL:** https://cset.georgetown.edu/wp-content/uploads/CSET-Cybersecurity-Risks-of-AI-Generated-Code.pdf
- **Data extracted:** 68% (InCoder) and 73% (Copilot) of code samples contained vulnerabilities. ChatGPT: only 5/21 programs initially secure.
- **Quality:** Georgetown policy research center.

**[18]** Gruda, D. & Aeon, B. (2025). "Seven Myths about AI and Productivity." *California Management Review*.
- **URL:** https://cmr.berkeley.edu/2025/10/seven-myths-about-ai-and-productivity-what-the-evidence-really-says/
- **Data extracted:** No robust meta-analytic relationship between AI adoption and aggregate productivity. AI is a "targeted accelerant" not blanket enhancer. 35% throughput lift for bottom-quartile reps, almost no gain for veterans.
- **Quality:** Meta-analytic review in top management journal.

**[19]** Anthropic (2025). "Estimating AI Productivity Gains."
- **URL:** https://www.anthropic.com/research/estimating-productivity-gains
- **Data extracted:** Claude achieves Spearman rho=0.44 vs developers' 0.50 for JIRA task duration estimation. 100K conversations analyzed. Software developers contribute 19% of total AI labor productivity gain.
- **Quality:** Methodologically rigorous but self-referential (Claude estimating Claude's value).

**[20]** Anthropic RCT on comprehension (cited via InfoQ/code4thought, 2026).
- **URL:** https://www.infoq.com/news/2026/02/ai-coding-skill-formation/
- **Data extracted:** N=52 software engineers. AI users scored 17% lower on comprehension quizzes (50% vs 67%). Largest declines in debugging comprehension.
- **Quality:** RCT. Referenced secondhand; original Anthropic publication not located.

---

## Industry Reports & Large-Scale Surveys

**[21]** Google DORA Report 2024. "Accelerate State of DevOps Report 2024."
- **URL:** https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/devops-sre/announcing-the-2024-dora-report
- **Data extracted:** Per 25% AI adoption increase: -1.5% delivery throughput, -7.2% delivery stability, +3.4% code quality, +7.5% documentation quality (from full report; not confirmed in summary blog post). 39% report little/no trust in AI code. ~3,000 respondents.
- **Quality:** Annual industry survey, established methodology.

**[22]** Google DORA Report 2025. "State of AI-Assisted Software Development."
- **URL:** https://dora.dev/research/2025/dora-report/
- **Data extracted:** AI adoption now positively correlated with throughput (reversal from 2024). Still negatively correlated with delivery stability. 90% use AI at work. Median 2 hrs/day interacting with AI (~25% of workday). 7 foundational AI capabilities model. ~5,000 respondents.
- **Quality:** Same methodology as 2024, larger sample.

**[23]** GitClear (2024/2025). "AI Copilot Code Quality Research."
- **URL:** https://www.gitclear.com/coding_on_copilot_data_shows_ais_downward_pressure_on_code_quality
- **Data extracted:** 211M lines analyzed. Code churn: 3.1% (2020) to 5.7% (2024). Copy/pasted code: 8.3% to 12.3%. Refactoring: 25% to <10%. Duplicated code blocks rose 8x.
- **Quality:** Largest known code change database. Correlation, not causation.

**[24]** Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2025.
- **URL:** https://survey.stackoverflow.co/2025/ai
- **Data extracted:** 84% use/plan to use AI. 51% daily. Trust: only 33% (down from 43% in 2024). 66% cite "almost right" as top frustration. 66% spend more time fixing AI code. 72% say vibe coding not part of professional work. 49K+ respondents.
- **Quality:** Large sample, annual survey. Self-reported.

**[25]** Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2024.
- **URL:** https://survey.stackoverflow.co/2024/
- **Data extracted:** 76% using/planning to use AI (up from 70%). 62% currently using. 45% say AI bad at complex tasks. 65K+ respondents.
- **Quality:** Same methodology, prior year baseline.

**[26]** DX AI-Assisted Engineering Q4 Impact Report (2025).
- **URL:** https://getdx.com/blog/ai-assisted-engineering-q4-impact-report-2025/
- **Data extracted:** Self-reported savings: 3.6 hrs/week average, 4.1 hrs/week for daily users. Junior: highest adoption (41.3% daily). Staff+: highest savings (4.4 hrs/week) but lowest adoption. 435 companies, 135K+ developers.
- **Quality:** Large sample. Self-reported (contradicted by METR [3]).

**[27]** Faros AI Productivity Paradox Report (2025).
- **URL:** https://www.faros.ai/blog/ai-software-engineering
- **Data extracted:** +21% tasks, +98% PRs, +154% PR size, +91% review time, +9% bugs. No DORA metric improvement at company level. Six studies converge on ~10% system-level ceiling. 10K+ devs, 1,255 teams.
- **Quality:** Large-scale telemetry. Vendor.

**[28]** Sonar "State of Code Developer Survey" (2026).
- **URL:** https://www.sonarsource.com/company/press-releases/sonar-data-reveals-critical-verification-gap-in-ai-coding/
- **Data extracted:** AI = 42% of committed code. 95% spend effort reviewing AI output. 38% say reviewing AI code harder than human code. 96% don't fully trust AI. 61% say AI produces code that "looks correct but isn't reliable." 1,100+ developers.
- **Quality:** Industry survey. Vendor.

**[29]** Cortex "Engineering in the Age of AI: 2026 Benchmark Report."
- **URL:** https://www.cortex.io/post/ai-is-making-engineering-faster-but-not-better-state-of-ai-benchmark-2026
- **Data extracted:** PRs/author +20%, cycle time +9%, deployment frequency +64%, incidents/PR +23.5%, change failure rate +30%. Only 32% have formal AI governance.
- **Quality:** 50+ engineering leaders surveyed. Self-selected platform users.

**[30]** Qodo "State of AI Code Quality 2025."
- **URL:** https://www.qodo.ai/reports/state-of-ai-code-quality/
- **Data extracted:** 65% say AI misses relevant context. 44% blame missing context for quality degradation. 88% low confidence shipping AI code. Only 3.8% report low hallucinations AND high confidence. N=609 developers.
- **Quality:** Industry survey. Vendor (Qodo sells AI code quality tools).

**[31]** CodeRabbit "State of AI vs Human Code Generation" (2025).
- **URL:** https://www.coderabbit.ai/blog/state-of-ai-vs-human-code-generation-report
- **Data extracted:** AI PRs produce 1.7x more issues (10.83 vs 6.45 per PR). Performance inefficiencies ~8x more. N=470 PRs (320 AI, 150 human).
- **Quality:** Moderate sample. Vendor (AI code review platform).

**[32]** Veracode GenAI Code Security Report (2025).
- **URL:** https://www.veracode.com/blog/genai-code-security-report/
- **Data extracted:** 45% of AI code fails security tests. 2.74x more vulnerabilities than human code. Java: 72% failure. XSS: 86% failure. 100+ LLMs, 80 tasks.
- **Quality:** Vendor report. Moderate benchmark size.

**[33]** IDC Report (2025). "How Do Software Developers Spend Their Time?"
- **URL:** https://www.infoworld.com/article/3831759/developers-spend-most-of-their-time-not-coding-idc-report.html
- **Data extracted:** Application development = only 16% of developer time (2024). Security rose from 8% to 13%.
- **Quality:** Established analyst firm. Full report behind paywall.

**[34]** Tidelift/SonarSource Developer Time Survey.
- **URL:** https://www.sonarsource.com/blog/how-much-time-do-developers-spend-actually-writing-code/
- **Data extracted:** 32% writing/improving code (39% for devs specifically). 12% testing. 19% maintenance. 23% meetings/ops.
- **Quality:** Industry survey. Self-reported.

**[35]** Atlassian "State of Developer Experience 2025."
- **URL:** https://www.atlassian.com/blog/developer/developer-experience-report-2025
- **Data extracted:** Developers spend only 16% coding (citing IDC). 10 hrs/week saved by AI, 10 hrs/week lost to inefficiencies. 3,500 developers across 6 countries.
- **Quality:** Large sample. Vendor survey.

**[36]** Rainforest QA "AI in Software Testing 2025."
- **URL:** https://www.rainforestqa.com/blog/ai-in-software-testing-report-2025
- **Data extracted:** 75% of automation teams adopted AI testing. 65-70% still in pilot phase. 80% test execution timeline reduction claimed. N=600+ developers.
- **Quality:** Vendor report. "10x productivity" claims are marketing.

**[37]** McKinsey (2023). "Unleashing Developer Productivity with Generative AI."
- **URL:** https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/tech-and-ai/our-insights/unleashing-developer-productivity-with-generative-ai
- **Data extracted:** Up to 2x faster on coding tasks. 20-45% of software engineering function impacted. N=40+ developers.
- **Quality:** Lab study, not field. Consulting firm.

**[38]** Bain & Company Technology Report (2025). "From Pilots to Payoff."
- **URL:** https://www.bain.com/insights/from-pilots-to-payoff-generative-ai-in-software-development-technology-report-2025/
- **Data extracted:** Coding + testing = 25-35% of SDLC. Basic AI assistants: 10-15% productivity boost. End-to-end transformation: 25-30%.
- **Quality:** Industry analysis. Key insight: coding is minority of SDLC.

**[39]** Greptile "State of AI Coding 2025."
- **URL:** https://www.greptile.com/state-of-ai-coding-2025
- **Data extracted:** Median PR size +33% (57 to 76 lines). LOC/developer: 4,450 to 7,839. Medium teams: 89% output increase. CLAUDE.md leads AI config adoption at 67%.
- **Quality:** Platform data (self-selected users). Vendor.

**[40]** AWS CodeWhisperer Productivity Challenge.
- **URL:** https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/amazon-codewhisperer-free-for-individual-use-is-now-generally-available/
- **Data extracted:** 27% more likely to complete tasks, 57% faster. Sample size/methodology undisclosed.
- **Quality:** Vendor marketing claim.

## Practitioner Analysis & Industry Commentary

**[41]** Osmani, A. (2024/2026). "The 70% Problem" and "Beyond Vibe Coding."
- **URL:** https://addyo.substack.com/p/the-70-problem-hard-truths-about
- **Data extracted:** AI produces 70% of solution rapidly; remaining 30% (edge cases, security, integration) is as hard as ever. Updated to 80/20 for early adopters (Jan 2026). "Code review is becoming the new bottleneck."
- **Quality:** Practitioner framework from Google engineering leader.

**[42]** Osmani, A. (2025/2026). "Conductors to Orchestrators: The Future of Agentic Coding." O'Reilly Radar.
- **URL:** https://www.oreilly.com/radar/conductors-to-orchestrators-the-future-of-agentic-coding/
- **Data extracted:** Conductor (micro-level, one agent) vs orchestrator (macro-level, multiple agents). Human effort front-loaded (specs) and back-loaded (review).
- **Quality:** Industry analysis. O'Reilly-published.

**[43]** Osmani, A. "Comprehension Debt" and "Code Review in the Age of AI."
- **URL:** https://addyosmani.com/blog/comprehension-debt/
- **Data extracted:** One team stopped all feature development for 3 weeks due to AI-generated code nobody understood. Osmani references Cortex data [29]: PRs 18% larger, incidents/PR +24%, change failure rates +30%.
- **Quality:** Practitioner observation. Quantitative metrics originate from Cortex [29], not Osmani's own measurement.

**[44]** Zakas, N. (2026). "From Coder to Orchestrator."
- **URL:** https://humanwhocodes.com/blog/2026/01/coder-orchestrator-future-software-engineering/
- **Data extracted:** Three-phase evolution: Coder (2024) -> Conductor (2025) -> Orchestrator (2025+). "Minimum Viable Engineering Team" (MVET) concept.
- **Quality:** Opinion piece from ESLint creator.

**[45]** Forret, P. (2025). "The Mythical Agent-Month: Brooks's Law in the Age of Agentic Software Development."
- **URL:** https://blog.forret.com/2025/2025-10-26/mythical-agent-month/
- **Data extracted:** AI as tool circumvents Brooks's Law (fewer humans needed). AI as autonomous agent strengthens Brooks's Law. Brooks's Surgical Team model maps to agentic workflows.
- **Quality:** Analysis/opinion building on Brooks's original work.

**[46]** Orosz, G. (2025/2026). *The Pragmatic Engineer Newsletter*.
- **URL:** https://newsletter.pragmaticengineer.com/p/the-future-of-software-engineering-with-ai
- **Data extracted:** Teams may shrink from 6-8 specialists to 3-4 full-stack engineers. Steve Yegge predicts ~50% engineering workforce reduction.
- **Quality:** Expert commentary. Partially paywalled.

**[47]** Vogels, W. (2025). AWS re:Invent keynote. "Verification Debt" concept.
- **URL:** https://cloudelligent.com/blog/werner-vogels-aws-reinvent-2025/
- **Data extracted:** "When the machine writes it, you'll have to rebuild that comprehension during review. That's what's called verification debt." "Vibe coding is fine, but only if you pay close attention."
- **Quality:** First-party keynote from Amazon CTO.

**[48]** Agarwal, A. (2025). "New Rules for Estimating Software Development Time in AI-era."
- **URL:** https://toashishagarwal.medium.com/new-rules-for-estimating-software-development-time-in-ai-era-460ec5347e1a
- **Data extracted:** COCOMO AI Augmentation Factor (AAF: 0.5-1.5). AI-Aware Three-Point Estimation. Task categorization by AI impact. Junior devs: up to 300% improvement on routine tasks.
- **Quality:** Blog post. AAF attribution to "a 2023 study" is unverifiable.

**[49]** Scientific American (2025). "Why Developers Using AI Are Working Longer Hours."
- **URL:** https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/why-developers-using-ai-are-working-longer-hours/
- **Data extracted:** Jevons Paradox applied to AI coding. 19.6% increase in out-of-hour commits. Developers took on more tasks, worked faster, worked longer hours.
- **Quality:** Journalistic reporting on UC Berkeley Haas School study.

**[50]** Thoughtworks Technology Radar Vol. 31/33 (2024/2025). "Complacency with AI-Generated Code."
- **URL:** https://www.thoughtworks.com/en-us/radar/techniques/complacency-with-ai-generated-code
- **Data extracted:** Rating: Hold. AI-driven confidence comes at expense of critical thinking. "Vibe coding has practically disappeared" by late 2025.
- **Quality:** Established industry radar.

## Context Engineering & Tools

**[51]** Anthropic (2025). "Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents."
- **URL:** https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-context-engineering-for-ai-agents
- **Data extracted:** Context engineering defined as evolution of prompt engineering. "Goldilocks zone" between too prescriptive and too vague. Compaction strategy. Structured note-taking for persistence.
- **Quality:** Primary source from model provider.

**[52]** Bockeler, B. (2026). "Context Engineering for Coding Agents." Martin Fowler's site.
- **URL:** https://martinfowler.com/articles/exploring-gen-ai/context-engineering-coding-agents.html
- **Data extracted:** "Everything is markdown." Codebase quality affects AI performance. "AI-friendly codebase design" concept.
- **Quality:** Established technical author platform.

**[53]** GitHub (2025). "Spec-driven development with AI" / spec-kit.
- **URL:** https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/generative-ai/spec-driven-development-with-ai-get-started-with-a-new-open-source-toolkit/
- **Data extracted:** Constitution -> Specify -> Plan -> Tasks workflow. "Intent as source of truth." Open-source toolkit.
- **Quality:** First-party from GitHub. Experimental status.

**[54]** HumanLayer (2025). "Advanced Context Engineering for Coding Agents (ACE-FCA)."
- **URL:** https://www.humanlayer.dev/blog/advanced-context-engineering
- **Data extracted:** Research -> Plan -> Implement workflow. Keep context window under 40% utilization. Frequent Intentional Compaction (FIC).
- **Quality:** Practitioner methodology. Vendor.

**[55]** Lutke, T. (Shopify CEO) & Karpathy, A. (2025). Context engineering definitions.
- **URL:** https://x.com/tobi/status/1935533422589399127
- **Data extracted:** "Context engineering" preferred over "prompt engineering." "The art of providing all the context for the task to be plausibly solvable."
- **Quality:** Industry leader statements.

**[56]** AGENTS.md Open Standard (2025). Linux Foundation / OpenAI.
- **URL:** https://agents.md/
- **Data extracted:** 60K+ projects, 25+ tools. 150 lines or fewer recommended. "90% of what you'd put in any tool-specific config is identical." OpenAI main repo has 88 AGENTS.md files.
- **Quality:** Open standard. Linux Foundation-stewarded.

**[57]** Red Hat (2025). "How spec-driven development improves AI coding quality."
- **URL:** https://developers.redhat.com/articles/2025/10/22/how-spec-driven-development-improves-ai-coding-quality
- **Data extracted:** Over 95% first-attempt accuracy with well-defined specs vs. 70-82% without.
- **Quality:** Practitioner guidance. 95% claim appears aspirational, not from controlled study.

**[58]** Chroma Research (2025). "Context Rot."
- **URL:** https://research.trychroma.com/context-rot
- **Data extracted:** "Models do not use their context uniformly; performance grows increasingly unreliable as input length grows." Performance scores drop to 60-70% at long contexts (not a fraction of usable window, but a performance degradation metric).
- **Quality:** Research from vector database company. Note: "60-70%" refers to performance score at long contexts, not a simple capacity fraction.

---

## Team Sizing & Organization

**[59]** AKF Partners (2025). "Engineering Team Sizes Are Evolving Rapidly with Agentic AI."
- **URL:** https://akfpartners.com/growth-blog/engineering-team-sizes-are-evolving-rapidly-with-agentic-AI-platforms-the-limits-challenges-and-principles-we-must-consider
- **Data extracted:** Traditional 6-10 -> AI-augmented 2-3 (MVP) or 4-6 (complex). "A single human developer paired with an AI agent could produce code at the pace of a small team."
- **Quality:** Expert analysis from scaling consultancy.

**[60]** Stack Overflow Blog (2025). "Beyond Code Generation: How AI Is Changing Tech Teams' Dynamics."
- **URL:** https://stackoverflow.blog/2025/10/06/beyond-code-generation-how-ai-is-changing-tech-teams-dynamics/
- **Data extracted:** 82% weekly AI use. 59% running 3+ AI tools in parallel. Google: breaking 30-60 person teams into smaller components. "2-3 developers can now build what once required 10+."
- **Quality:** Journalistic reporting with industry interviews.

**[61]** OpenAI (2025). "Building an AI-Native Engineering Team."
- **URL:** https://developers.openai.com/codex/guides/build-ai-native-engineering-team
- **Data extracted:** Codex team: ~40 people (1 PM, 2 designers, ~37 engineers). Multi-agent orchestration roles defined. "The primary job became enabling agents to do useful work."
- **Quality:** First-party documentation.

**[62]** Scrum.org Forum. "How to approach story point estimation with AI Dev acceleration tools?"
- **URL:** https://www.scrum.org/forum/scrum-forum/94752/how-approach-story-point-estimation-advent-ai-dev-acceleration-tools
- **Data extracted:** Estimate complexity not time. Keep estimation tool-agnostic. Use empirical velocity. Consider throughput/cycle time over story points.
- **Quality:** Community discussion, not formal research.

---

## Estimation Tools

**[63]** Devtimate.
- **URL:** https://devtimate.com/
- **Data extracted:** Generates estimates from uploaded requirements. Claimed 85-90% initial accuracy, 95%+ after review. Jira integration.
- **Quality:** Vendor claims, not independently validated.

**[64]** CostGPT.ai.
- **URL:** https://costgpt.ai/
- **Data extracted:** Trained on 2,000+ projects. Claimed 80%+ accuracy. $19 one-time fee.
- **Quality:** Vendor claims.

**[65]** Quanter/QuanterIA.
- **URL:** https://www.quanter.com/en/
- **Data extracted:** LLM-based Function Point Analysis from natural language. ISO/IEC compliant (IFPUG, NESMA). Presented at ISMA 22.
- **Quality:** Industry-recognized FPA tool.

**[66]** Intelligent Story Point Estimation (Jira Plugin, nVision Data).
- **URL:** https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1234430/intelligent-story-point-estimation
- **Data extracted:** ML/AI analysis of past Jira data. Fuzzy matching for similar issues.
- **Quality:** Marketplace plugin since May 2024.

**[67]** EstiMate AI (Jira Plugin).
- **URL:** https://estimate.solutions/
- **Data extracted:** Data-driven time estimates from historical Jira data. One team halved sprint planning time.
- **Quality:** Vendor claims.

**[68]** Zenhub AI Sprint Planning.
- **URL:** https://www.zenhub.com/
- **Data extracted:** Historical Git data for predictive estimation. Claims 50% reduction in planning poker sessions. Dapper Labs: 40% increase in epic completion rates.
- **Quality:** Vendor claims. GitHub-native.

**[69]** CodeScene.
- **URL:** https://codescene.com/
- **Data extracted:** Behavioral code analysis. Files with alert-level Code Health have 15x more defects and require 124% more dev time.
- **Quality:** Validated methodology. Founded by Adam Tornhill.

**[70]** Claude Code Issue #21449 (2026). Effort estimation feature request rejected.
- **URL:** https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/21449
- **Data extracted:** Claude Code deliberately blocks estimation. Closed as NOT_PLANNED. Confirms Anthropic product decision.
- **Quality:** Primary source (GitHub issue).

**[71]** Valletta Software (2025). LLM comparison for project estimation.
- **URL:** https://vallettasoftware.com/blog/post/comparing-chatgpt-claude-and-deepseek-in-automating-project-specification-estimates-when-requirements-change
- **Data extracted:** Claude 3.7 outperformed GPT-4o and DeepSeek V3 for change estimation. Recommends AI-generated first draft + human validation.
- **Quality:** Blog post comparison. Single evaluation.

---

## Non-Code Work & CI/CD

**[72]** Code review time studies (Codacy/JetBrains/Stack Overflow).
- **URL:** https://blog.codacy.com/10-facts-about-code-reviews-and-quality
- **Data extracted:** Average ~5 hrs/week on code reviews (10-12.5% of work week). Spending >1 day/week reviewing does NOT improve perceived quality.
- **Quality:** Aggregated survey data.

**[73]** Agile sprint overhead analysis (Connolly).
- **URL:** https://medium.com/serious-scrum/how-long-is-a-developer-day-b5a2e4ab1e1c
- **Data extracted:** Scrum events alone reduce productive time by ~25%. Typical productive time: 5-6 hrs/day. 44.45% consider dailies ineffective.
- **Quality:** Practitioner analysis.

**[74]** Testing effort ratios (multiple sources).
- **URL:** https://hypersense-software.com/blog/2025/07/19/software-development-effort-allocation-dev-qa-design-pm-ratio/
- **Data extracted:** QA/testing: 10-60% of total effort by project type. Common baseline: testing = 40% of development effort. Microsoft: 1:1 tester-to-developer.
- **Quality:** Aggregated industry data.

**[75]** CI/CD pipeline metrics (JetBrains/GitLab/CircleCI).
- **URL:** https://www.jetbrains.com/teamcity/ci-cd-guide/devops-ci-cd-metrics/
- **Data extracted:** CI/CD reduces time-to-market by 33%. 60% higher team efficiency. 50% reduction in change failure rate.
- **Quality:** Vendor-published benchmarks.

**[76]** Bug0 (2026). "The 2026 Quality Tax."
- **URL:** https://bug0.com/blog/the-2026-quality-tax-ai-assisted-development-qa-budget
- **Data extracted:** Manual QA creates $55K-$78K in hidden costs per developer annually. AI-integrated stacks have harder context recovery and more frequent test maintenance.
- **Quality:** Vendor report. Methodology not transparent.

---

## Context Debt & Benchmarks

**[77]** Context Debt (multiple sources, 2025-2026).
- **URL:** https://www.mpt.solutions/beyond-comprehension-debt-why-context-architecture-is-the-real-ai-moat/
- **Data extracted:** "AI generates at 10x velocity, context dissipates at 10x rate." The "repetition tax" of re-entering context per interaction.
- **Quality:** Practitioner analysis from multiple sources.

**[78]** HumanEval / SWE-bench benchmark scores (2025-2026).
- **URL:** https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/humaneval
- **Data extracted:** HumanEval: top models 90-96% (near-saturated, data leakage documented). SWE-bench Verified: top at 80.9%, average across 77 models at 62.2%. 20-31 pp drop from HumanEval to HumanEvalNext.
- **Quality:** Benchmark aggregation. HumanEval reliability questioned.

**[79]** Non-determinism in AI output (multiple sources).
- **URL:** https://www.statsig.com/perspectives/what-are-non-deterministic-ai-outputs-
- **Data extracted:** Even temperature=0 doesn't guarantee identical output. Breaks traditional testing/QA paradigms. Same prompt may produce different code.
- **Quality:** Technical analysis.

**[80]** Microsoft (2025). "Taxonomy of Failure Modes in Agentic AI Systems."
- **URL:** https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2025/04/24/new-whitepaper-outlines-the-taxonomy-of-failure-modes-in-ai-agents/
- **Data extracted:** Four failure categories: Goal Misalignment, Boundary Violation, Context Loss, Capability Overestimation. Agents attempt tasks beyond abilities "with confidence that masks limitations."
- **Quality:** Microsoft AI Red Team whitepaper.

**[81]** ZenML (2025). "What 1,200 Production Deployments Reveal About LLMOps."
- **URL:** https://www.zenml.io/blog/what-1200-production-deployments-reveal-about-llmops-in-2025
- **Data extracted:** 2023 was prompt engineering; 2024-2025 is context engineering. Agent infrastructure in active flux with "parallel experimentation rather than industry convergence."
- **Quality:** First-party operational data.

**[82]** "18-Month Wall" pattern / Ox Security (2025).
- **URL:** https://addyo.substack.com/p/the-reality-of-ai-assisted-software
- **Data extracted:** Months 1-3: euphoria. Months 4-9: plateau. Months 10-15: decline. Months 16-18: stall. 300+ repos analyzed, 10 anti-patterns in 80-100% of AI code.
- **Quality:** Observational/anecdotal framework. Treat with caution.

**[83]** Anthropic Internal Study (2025). "How AI Is Transforming Work at Anthropic."
- **URL:** https://www.anthropic.com/news/how-ai-is-transforming-work-at-anthropic (Note: distinct from [19] which covers the conversation-analysis methodology)
- **Data extracted:** 132 engineers surveyed, 200K transcripts. Self-reported +50% productivity. 59% AI usage. 27% work wouldn't have been done without AI. +67% PRs/day.
- **Quality:** Internal study. Self-referential methodology.

**[84]** ACONIC Framework (2025). "An Approach for Systematic Decomposition of Complex LLM Tasks." arXiv:2510.07772.
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2510.07772v1
- **Data extracted:** Models tasks as constraint satisfaction (3-SAT). Defines "frontiers of difficulty" separating LLM-solvable from LLM-unsolvable tasks.
- **Quality:** Academic. Principled complexity measurement.
